Internal Incident Report — Bayan Capital Holdings, Inc. (PSE issuer) — 03 June 2026 — For internal use; pending SEC/PSE disclosure determination.
Summary: A cyber incident on 02 June affected our treasury workstation and HR payroll portal hosted by Lakbay Cloud Services, leading to temporary isolation under our information barrier protocol.
Indicators show unauthorized access via a supplier VPN credential and deployment of “NightPal” ransomware, with no confirmed exfiltration; NPC notification assessment under the Data Privacy Act is in progress.
Potential PII exposure includes employee names and corporate email aliases only; no BIR TINs, bank account numbers, or government IDs were stored in the affected segment.
We have activated the Insider List and imposed confidentiality per SRC and PSE rules, restricting knowledge to the Cyber SIT members and designated insiders.
A trading blackout window is imposed for all Covered Persons of Bayan Capital and its subsidiary Huminga Payments Corp. until incident details are publicly disclosed.
Concurrently, a draft term sheet for a peso-denominated sustainability-linked note and a pilot custodial service for digital payment tokens under a BSP sandbox were stored on the same server but remain encrypted; contents are non-public and commercially sensitive.
Selective disclosure risk: counterparties Kambas Syndicate Fund and Oragon Trust Bank are not aware of the incident; external communication is limited to need-to-know with wall-cross procedures.
No customer wallets or on-chain assets were impacted; our operations team confirms that “crypto” references in the training module refer to generic educational content and not to live product data.
Environmental/ESG Note: the 2025 GHG inventory recalculation factors are being updated; this operational workstream uses “materiality” in a sustainability context unrelated to securities MNPI.
Control actions: segmented the treasury VLAN, rotated all privileged credentials via Talaan PAM, applied IOC blocks at the Batak SOC, and engaged Bulalakaw Forensics for imaging.
Preliminary financial impact is below the 5% revenue threshold; however, Legal advises this may be material in light of concurrent capital markets activity.
All staff are reminded that tips to friends, family, or chat groups about the incident, the sustainability-linked note, or the BSP sandbox are prohibited and will be investigated.
For process clarity: the marketing team may continue scheduled ESG awareness posts so long as no incident details, counterparties, or commercial terms are referenced.
Point of contact: Atty. Celina V. Marikit, Compliance Officer, celina.marikit@bayancapital.ph, +63 28 555 2040.
Report prepared by Mr. Ramon S. Dalisay, Head of Risk, with support from Ms. Lira P. Alonzo (HR) and Engr. Paolo J. Davao (IT Infrastructure).
